title: ArcForge Trusted Execution Contract Bundle V0
description: Draft machine-readable contracts for the G0.5 trusted execution boundary. Contract schema version 0 targets protocol major 1.

DecimalUint.$comment: Unsigned 64-bit decimal. JSON Schema limits lexical form and width; the review/runtime validator MUST reject values above 18446744073709551615.

NonZeroDecimalUint.$comment: Non-zero unsigned 64-bit decimal. JSON Schema limits lexical form and width; the review/runtime validator MUST reject values above 18446744073709551615.

CanonicalHttpsOrigin.$comment: ASCII lowercase DNS origin only: no userinfo, path, query, fragment, IP literal, localhost, trailing dot, or default :443. The semantic validator performs trusted URI parsing, port range checks, IDNA/canonical round-trip, and SSRF policy checks.

CommandPayloadRef.$comment: The referenced payload is accepted only when schema_id/schema_version are present in the negotiated closed registry.

CommandEnvelope.$comment: command_id plus payload_hash is the idempotency identity. payload_hash is the domain-separated RFC 8785 hash of the complete CommandPayloadRef (schema_id, schema_version, and body ContentRef), using object type CommandPayloadRef and schema version 0; it is not merely body.sha256. issued_at is audit-only and never authorizes execution.

ToolIntent.$comment: This object is an untrusted proposal. It is never executable and never an authorization. causation_command_id and correlation_id preserve the authoritative Command-to-Intent lineage.

ClockBinding.$comment: Execution validity requires both the trusted monotonic deadline and trusted UTC checks to pass. A clock epoch change invalidates the binding.

DataBoundaryGrant.$comment: Any source, classification, provider, model, endpoint, redirect, egress, or consent change invalidates this grant before provider invocation.

NewResourceIdentity.$comment: The parent identity and absence proof must be rechecked immediately before apply; string containment is never an authorization fallback.

ResourceOperationSet.$comment: Derived hash input only. resources MUST be sorted by resource_operation_id before RFC 8785 canonicalization; the review validator enforces ordering and uniqueness.

InvocationSpec.$comment: source_tool_intent_id and source_intent_hash bind this sealed object to the untrusted proposal it normalized. spec_hash uses RFC 8785 JCS over this object without spec_hash, prefixed by the ArcForge canonicalization-version, object-type, and schema-version domain separator. Resources are sorted by resource_operation_id before hashing. expected_resource_set_hash is the domain-separated hash of {resources:[...]} after that sort, using object type ResourceOperationSet and schema version 0. canonical_args.sha256 and exact_args_bytes_sha256 bind the same exact byte sequence and MUST be equal; the review validator enforces these cross-field invariants.

PolicyDecision.$comment: Allow waives only the human approval step; a closed trusted PreviewBinding and AuthorizationGrant remain mandatory for every effect.

PreviewBinding.$comment: Trusted Renderer input and hash binding. Provider prose or HTML is never authoritative preview data; unknown fields are rejected before rendering or approval.

Approval.$comment: The approval binds the exact user-visible preview and sealed InvocationSpec. Version or hash changes require a new approval.

AuthorizationGrant.$comment: Every effect requires this one-use grant and the exact trusted preview, even when policy waives human approval. Execution revalidates its hashes, lifetime, policy, resource revisions, broker epoch, durable fencing high-water, and active status.

SanitizedReceiptRef.$comment: Only sanitized receipt bytes may be persisted. Raw provider/Broker receipt bytes remain inside the TCB long enough to verify, redact, scan, hash, and discard; a clean attestation is necessary but still requires G1 canary tests.

ResourceOutcome.$comment: Optional after-state and receipt fields are observations only; they never override outcome. Applied requires all success evidence. NotApplied requires a trusted receipt or structured error. Conflict requires an observed current identity. StillUnknown may retain partial evidence but cannot be promoted by field presence.

ReceiptSource.$comment: V0 accepts receipt evidence only after the Broker verifies and attests it inside the TCB. attestation_hash binds the verified source bytes; verified_by_broker_epoch_id identifies the Broker epoch that made the trust decision. Detached/provider signatures remain FeatureDisabled until their algorithm, preimage, key trust, and rotation contracts are frozen.

EffectReceipt.$comment: The expected resource set must exactly equal the sealed InvocationSpec set. Resource outcomes are sorted by resource_operation_id before canonical hashing; missing, duplicate, or extra operations invalidate the receipt and preserve Unknown.

expected_resource_set_hash.$comment: Must exactly equal the sealed InvocationSpec expected_resource_set_hash; an unequal, missing, duplicate, or extra resource operation invalidates the receipt and preserves Unknown.
